steps.title: Isolate 4x from the given equation
steps.content:
Starting with $4x - \frac{1}{2} = -5$, add $\frac{1}{2}$ to both sides:
$$4x = -5 + \frac{1}{2}$$
Convert to a common denominator:
$$4x = -\frac{10}{2} + \frac{1}{2} = -\frac{9}{2}$$

steps.title: Express 8x − 1 in terms of 4x and substitute
steps.content:
Notice that $8x - 1 = 2(4x) - 1$. Substitute $4x = -\frac{9}{2}$:
$$8x - 1 = 2 \cdot \left(-\frac{9}{2}\right) - 1 = -9 - 1 = -10$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
